# AI assets in game development (2026-02-16 12:53Z)

_**User**_

"AI Art Generation

"

this strikes a nerve for many. it's not ai art. it's ai assets right. you can research development/publicsummerengine Studio tab to see how we do. but essentially users can create assets, upload and organize their own existing ones, and then have Summer import any asset directly into their games on command

---

_**Agent (model composer-1.5, mode Agent)**_

                  "content": "title: \"AI Art Generation\""

                {
                  "lineNumber": 7,
                  "content": "## AI Art for Prototyping"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 9,
                  "content": "Summer's AI art generation is designed specifically for **game development prototyping**. It's not about creating final production art—it's about getting your game playable and testable quickly."
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 12,
                  "content": "- AI art works best for simple, clear concepts"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 17,
                  "content": "**What AI Art Does Well:**"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 199,
                  "content": "**Pro Tip**: Use AI art to get your game playable quickly, then gradually replace key assets with human-created art as you approach release. This hybrid approach lets you validate your game concept without upfront art costs."

        "contents": "---\ntitle: \"Summer Asset Store\"\ndescription: \"Browse, search, and discover game assets created by the Summer community\"\nicon: \"images\"\n---\n\n## Human-Created Art Community\n\nThe Summer Asset Store showcases **human-created game assets** from our community of artists and developers. We believe AI has a place in prototyping, but human creativity produces the art that makes games memorable.\n\n**What You'll Find:**\n- Professional-quality sprites and animations\n- Hand-crafted textures and materials\n- UI elements with attention to detail\n- Unique artistic styles and interpretations\n\n**Our Standards:**\n- All assets are created by real artists\n- Quality reviews ensure professional standards\n- Clear licensing for commercial use\n- Support for artists through fair compensation\n\n## Accessing the Summer Asset Store\n\n&lt;CardGroup cols={2}&gt;\n  &lt;Card title=\"In Summer Editor\" icon=\"desktop\"&gt;\n    Navigate to Art → Browse Asset Store in the Summer application\n  &lt;/Card&gt;\n  &lt;Card title=\"Web Browser\" icon=\"globe\"&gt;\n    Visit store.summerengine.com to browse online\n  &lt;/Card&gt;\n&lt;/CardGroup&gt;\n\n## Browsing Assets\n\n### Filter by Asset Type\n\nUse the filter panel to narrow down your search:\n\n- **2D Images**: General artwork, concept art, backgrounds\n- **Sprites**: Character sprites, animations, game objects\n- **Textures**: Seamless textures, materials, surface patterns\n- **UI Elements**: Buttons, icons, panels, HUD components\n- **3D Models**: Characters, props, environment pieces\n\n### Filter by Creation Method\n\nWe believe in transparency, so every asset is clearly labeled:\n\n- **Human-Created**: Assets made by real artists with their unique creativity and style\n- **AI-Generated**: Assets created using AI tools, perfect for prototyping and placeholders\n- **Hybrid**: Assets that combine AI generation with human refinement and editing\n\n### Search Functionality\n\nThe search system looks through:\n- Asset titles and descriptions\n- Creator names and profiles\n- Tags and categories\n- File metadata\n\n**Search Tips:**\n- Use specific terms: \"medieval sword\" instead of just \"weapon\"",

        "contents": "- **UI Graphics**: Buttons, icons, panels, HUD elements\n- **Concept Art**: Character designs, environment concepts\n\n### 3D Assets\n- **Models**: Characters, props, environment pieces\n- **Textures**: PBR materials, normal maps, diffuse textures\n- **Archives**: Multi-file asset packages\n\n### Supported Formats\n\n**Images**: PNG, JPEG, WebP, GIF, SVG  \n**3D Models**: OBJ, GLTF/GLB, FBX, Blend, DAE  \n**Textures**: TGA, HDR  \n**Archives**: ZIP, RAR (for multi-file assets)\n\n&lt;Info&gt;\n**File Size Limit**: Up to 500MB per asset to support high-quality game development needs.\n&lt;/Info&gt;\n\n## Getting Started\n\n&lt;Steps&gt;\n  &lt;Step title=\"Access the Art System\"&gt;\n    In Summer, navigate to the Art menu or visit the online gallery at summerengine.com/art\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n  &lt;Step title=\"Browse Assets\"&gt;\n    Explore the community gallery using filters, search, and categories\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n  &lt;Step title=\"Create Your First Asset\"&gt;\n    Use the AI generation tool to create custom art for your game\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n  &lt;Step title=\"Build Collections\"&gt;\n    Save your favorite assets to collections for easy organization\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n&lt;/Steps&gt;\n\n## Art Styles Available\n\nWhen generating AI art, choose from these optimized styles:\n\n&lt;Columns cols={3}&gt;\n  &lt;Card title=\"Realistic\" icon=\"camera\"&gt;\n    Photorealistic renders and detailed artwork\n  &lt;/Card&gt;\n  &lt;Card title=\"Cartoon\" icon=\"face-smile\"&gt;\n    Stylized, family-friendly cartoon aesthetics\n  &lt;/Card&gt;\n  &lt;Card title=\"Anime\" icon=\"sparkles\"&gt;\n    Japanese animation-inspired character art\n  &lt;/Card&gt;\n  &lt;Card title=\"Pixel Art\" icon=\"grid\"&gt;\n    Retro pixel-perfect sprites and textures\n  &lt;/Card&gt;\n  &lt;Card title=\"Low-Poly\" icon=\"cube\"&gt;\n    Minimalist 3D-style with flat colors\n  &lt;/Card&gt;\n  &lt;Card title=\"Fantasy\" icon=\"dragon\"&gt;\n    Magical, medieval, and fantastical themes\n  &lt;/Card&gt;\n&lt;/Columns&gt;\n\n## Supporting the Dev Community\n\n### Transparency First\n- **Clear Labeling**: Every asset shows \"AI-Generated\" or \"Human-Created\" prominently\n- **Creation Method**: See exactly how each asset was made\n- **Artist Attribution**: Human artists get full credit and recognition\n- **AI Disclosure**: AI-generated content is never hidden or misrepresented",

        "contents": "### By Development Stage\nOrganize by when you'll use them:\n- \"Prototype Assets\"\n- \"Production Ready\"\n- \"Polish Phase\"\n- \"Marketing Materials\"\n\n## Managing Collections\n\n### Collection Settings\nEach collection has configurable options:\n\n**Visibility**\n- **Public**: Anyone can view and follow your collection\n- **Private**: Only you can see and access the collection\n\n**Collaboration** (Coming Soon)\n- **Team Shared**: Available to your team members\n- **Read-Only**: Others can view but not modify\n- **Full Access**: Team members can add/remove assets\n\n### Editing Collections\n- **Rename**: Update collection names as your projects evolve\n- **Reorder**: Drag and drop to organize assets within collections\n- **Remove Assets**: Clean up collections by removing outdated assets\n- **Merge Collections**: Combine similar collections to reduce clutter\n\n## Sharing Collections\n\n### Public Collections\nWhen you make a collection public:\n- Other users can discover it in the community section\n- People can follow your collection to get updates\n- Your collection appears on your public profile\n- Others can fork your collection (create their own copy)\n\n### Collection URLs\nEach public collection gets a shareable URL:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nsummerengine.com/art/collections/username/collection-name\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nShare these links with:\n- Team members and collaborators\n- Community forums and social media\n- Game development blogs and tutorials\n\n## Using Collections in Projects\n\n### Direct Import\nImport entire collections into Summer projects:\n1. Right-click on a collection in Summer\n2. Select \"Import All to Project\"\n3. Choose which project folder to import to\n4. Assets maintain their organization structure\n\n### Smart Suggestions\nSummer's AI uses your collections to make better suggestions:\n- Recommends similar assets based on your collection patterns\n- Suggests complementary assets for your existing collections\n- Learns your style preferences from collection choices\n\n### Project Templates\nCreate reusable asset packages:\n1. Build a collection with all assets for a specific game type\n2. Use it as a template for new projects\n3. Share templates with the community",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [

            "lineNumber": 78
          },
          {
            "text": "### Collection Settings",
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "text": "Each collection has configurable options:",
            "lineNumber": 80
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81
          },
          {
            "text": "**Visibility**",
            "lineNumber": 82
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Public**: Anyone can view and follow your collection",
            "lineNumber": 83
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Private**: Only you can see and access the collection",
            "lineNumber": 84
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 85
          },
          {
            "text": "**Collaboration** (Coming Soon)",
            "lineNumber": 86
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Team Shared**: Available to your team members",
            "lineNumber": 87
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Read-Only**: Others can view but not modify",
            "lineNumber": 88
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Full Access**: Team members can add/remove assets",
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90
          },
          {
            "text": "### Editing Collections",
            "lineNumber": 91
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Rename**: Update collection names as your projects evolve",
            "lineNumber": 92
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Reorder**: Drag and drop to organize assets within collections",
            "lineNumber": 93
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Remove Assets**: Clean up collections by removing outdated assets",
            "lineNumber": 94
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Merge Collections**: Combine similar collections to reduce clutter",
            "lineNumber": 95
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96
          },
          {
            "text": "## Sharing Collections",
            "lineNumber": 97
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98
          },
          {
            "text": "### Public Collections",
            "lineNumber": 99
          },
          {
            "text": "When you make a collection public:",
            "lineNumber": 100
          },
          {
            "text": "- Other users can discover it in the community section",
            "lineNumber": 101
          },
          {
            "text": "- People can follow your collection to get updates",
            "lineNumber": 102
          },
          {
            "text": "- Your collection appears on your public profile",
            "lineNumber": 103
          },
          {
            "text": "- Others can fork your collection (create their own copy)",
            "lineNumber": 104
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 105
          },
          {
            "text": "### Collection URLs",
            "lineNumber": 106
          },
          {
            "text": "Each public collection gets a shareable URL:",
            "lineNumber": 107
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 108
          },
          {
            "text": "summerengine.com/art/collections/username/collection-name",
            "lineNumber": 109
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 110
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 111
          },
          {
            "text": "Share these links with:",
            "lineNumber": 112
          },
          {
            "text": "- Team members and collaborators",
            "lineNumber": 113
          },
          {
            "text": "- Community forums and social media",
            "lineNumber": 114
          },
          {
            "text": "- Game development blogs and tutorials",
            "lineNumber": 115
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116
          },
          {
            "text": "## Using Collections in Projects",
            "lineNumber": 117
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118
          },
          {
            "text": "### Direct Import",
            "lineNumber": 119
          },
          {
            "text": "Import entire collections into Summer projects:",
            "lineNumber": 120
          },
          {
            "text": "1. Right-click on a collection in Summer",
            "lineNumber": 121
          },
          {
            "text": "2. Select \"Import All to Project\"",
            "lineNumber": 122
          },
          {
            "text": "3. Choose which project folder to import to",
            "lineNumber": 123
          },
          {
            "text": "4. Assets maintain their organization structure",
            "lineNumber": 124
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 125
          },
          {
            "text": "### Smart Suggestions",
            "lineNumber": 126
          },
          {
            "text": "Summer's AI uses your collections to make better suggestions:",
            "lineNumber": 127
          },
          {
            "text": "- Recommends similar assets based on your collection patterns",
            "lineNumber": 128
          },
          {
            "text": "- Suggests complementary assets for your existing collections",
            "lineNumber": 129
          },
          {
            "text": "- Learns your style preferences from collection choices",
            "lineNumber": 130
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 131
          },
          {
            "text": "### Project Templates",
            "lineNumber": 132
          },
          {
            "text": "Create reusable asset packages:",
            "lineNumber": 133
          },
          {
            "text": "1. Build a collection with all assets for a specific game type",
            "lineNumber": 134
          },
          {
            "text": "2. Use it as a template for new projects",
            "lineNumber": 135
          },
          {
            "text": "3. Share templates with the community",
            "lineNumber": 136
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.28715428709983826
    },

      "codeBlock": {

        "range": {

          "endPosition": {
            "line": 71

        "contents": "---\ntitle: \"Asset Collections\"\ndescription: \"Organize and manage your favorite game assets with collections\"\nicon: \"folder\"\n---\n\n## What are Collections?\n\nCollections are personalized folders that help you organize and manage your favorite assets from the Summer Asset Store. Think of them as curated playlists for game assets - you can create collections for specific projects, art styles, or asset types.\n\n## Creating Collections\n\n&lt;Steps&gt;\n  &lt;Step title=\"Access Collections\"&gt;\n    Go to Art → My Collections in Summer or visit your profile on summerengine.com/art\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n  &lt;Step title=\"Create New Collection\"&gt;\n    Click \"New Collection\" and give it a descriptive name\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n  &lt;Step title=\"Set Visibility\"&gt;\n    Choose whether your collection is Public (visible to all) or Private (only you can see it)\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n  &lt;Step title=\"Add Description\"&gt;\n    Write a brief description explaining what the collection contains\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n&lt;/Steps&gt;\n\n## Adding Assets to Collections\n\n### From the Asset Store\nWhen browsing assets in the gallery:\n1. Click the bookmark icon on any asset\n2. Select which collection(s) to add it to\n3. Create new collections on-the-fly if needed\n\n### From Asset Pages\nOn individual asset pages:\n1. Click \"Add to Collection\"\n2. Choose from existing collections or create a new one\n3. Assets are saved instantly\n\n### Bulk Operations\nSelect multiple assets and add them to collections in batch:\n1. Use checkboxes to select multiple assets\n2. Click \"Add Selected to Collection\"\n3. Choose your target collection\n\n## Collection Organization Ideas\n\n### By Project\nCreate collections for each of your games:\n- \"Medieval RPG Assets\"\n- \"Space Shooter Graphics\"\n- \"Puzzle Game UI Elements\"\n\n### By Asset Type\nOrganize by the type of content:\n- \"Character Sprites\"\n- \"Environment Textures\"\n- \"UI Components\"\n- \"Sound Effects\"\n\n### By Art Style\nGroup assets with similar aesthetics:\n- \"Pixel Art Collection\"\n- \"Low-Poly Models\"\n- \"Hand-Drawn Illustrations\"\n- \"Realistic Textures\"\n\n### By Development Stage\nOrganize by when you'll use them:",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./publishing/steam.mdx",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 149
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 220
          }
        },
        "contents": "&lt;/Warning&gt;\n\n---\n\n## Steam Features Integration (Optional)\n\nSummer Engine supports Steamworks integration for advanced features:\n\n### GodotSteam Plugin\n\n[GodotSteam](https://godotsteam.com/) is a community plugin that adds:\n- ✅ Achievements\n- ✅ Leaderboards  \n- ✅ Steam Cloud saves\n- ✅ Steam Input (controller support)\n- ✅ Steam Overlay\n- ✅ Multiplayer matchmaking\n\n**How to add**:\n1. Download GodotSteam from [godotsteam.com](https://godotsteam.com/)\n2. Extract to your project's &#96;addons/&#96; folder\n3. Enable in **Project → Project Settings → Plugins**\n4. Follow GodotSteam docs for achievement/leaderboard setup\n\n&lt;Info&gt;\n  GodotSteam works **identically** in Summer Engine - all Godot Steam tutorials apply directly.\n&lt;/Info&gt;\n\n---\n\n## Platform-Specific Configuration\n\n### Windows Build Settings\n\n**Binary Format → Embed PCK**: ✅ **On**\n- Creates single &#96;.exe&#96; file (easier for Steam)\n- Slightly slower loading than separate &#96;.pck&#96; (negligible for most games)\n\n**Debug → Export Console Wrapper**:\n- **Debug Only** - For testing (console window shows debug output)\n- **No** - For final release (no console window)\n\n**Application → Product Name**: Your game's name as shown in Task Manager\n\n### macOS Build Settings\n\n**Binary Format → Architecture**: &#96;universal&#96; ✅\n- Supports both Intel and Apple Silicon Macs\n- Slightly larger file size but maximum compatibility\n\n**Application → Bundle Identifier**: Set your unique ID (e.g., &#96;com.yourstudio.yourgame&#96;)\n\n&lt;Tip&gt;\n  Use the **same bundle identifier** across all your game versions (Mac, iOS if applicable). This is important for cloud save continuity.\n&lt;/Tip&gt;\n\n### Linux Build (Steam Deck)\n\n**Binary Format → Architecture**: &#96;x86_64&#96;\n- Steam Deck uses this architecture\n- **Export → Texture Format**: Use S3TC BPTC (same as Windows)\n\n**Steam Deck Optimization**:\n- Test UI at 1280x800 resolution (Steam Deck native)\n- Support controllers (Steam Input handles this)\n- Optimize for ~3-4 hours battery life\n\n---\n\n## Launch Options in Steamworks",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "&lt;/Warning&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 150
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 151
          },

            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 152
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 159

          {
            "text": "[GodotSteam](https://godotsteam.com/) is a community plugin that adds:",
            "lineNumber": 160
          },
          {
            "text": "- ✅ Achievements",
            "lineNumber": 161
          },
          {
            "text": "- ✅ Leaderboards  ",
            "lineNumber": 162

          {
            "text": "- ✅ Steam Cloud saves",
            "lineNumber": 163

            "lineNumber": 166

            "lineNumber": 167
          },
          {
            "text": "**How to add**:",

            "text": "1. Download GodotSteam from [godotsteam.com](https://godotsteam.com/)",

            "text": "2. Extract to your project's &#96;addons/&#96; folder",

          },
          {
            "text": "3. Enable in **Project → Project Settings → Plugins**",
            "lineNumber": 171
          },
          {
            "text": "4. Follow GodotSteam docs for achievement/leaderboard setup",
            "lineNumber": 172
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 173
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;Info&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 174
          },
          {
            "text": "  GodotSteam works **identically** in Summer Engine - all Godot Steam tutorials apply directly.",
            "lineNumber": 175
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/Info&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 176
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 177
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 178
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 179
          },
          {

          {
            "text": "&lt;Tip&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 202

          {
            "text": "  Use the **same bundle identifier** across all your game versions (Mac, iOS if applicable). This is important for cloud save continuity.",
            "lineNumber": 203

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./ai-tools/operations.mdx",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 42
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 94
          }
        },
        "contents": "### What Summer Can Build For You\n\nSummer doesn't just talk about game development - it actively builds things in your project. Here's what it can create:\n\n**Game Code That Works**\nSummer writes production-ready GDScript that integrates with your existing codebase. Not just snippets, but complete systems:\n\n- Character controllers with proper physics integration\n- Inventory systems with UI and data persistence\n- AI behaviors that work with your game's mechanics\n- Network synchronization for multiplayer features\n\n**Visual Assets You Need**\nWhen you need game art but don't have an artist yet:\n\n- UI textures and button graphics\n- Environment backgrounds and tilesets\n- Character sprites and animations\n- Special effects and particle textures\n\n**Audio That Fits Your Game**\nSound design that's tailored to your game's atmosphere:\n\n- Background music that matches your game's mood\n- Sound effects for specific game actions\n- Ambient audio loops for different environments\n- Voice acting prompts for professional recording\n\n**Documentation That Helps**\nSummer creates living documentation that stays current:\n\n- Code comments that explain complex systems\n- Design documents that capture your game vision\n- Technical guides for your team members\n- Player-facing content like tutorials and lore\n\n### Summer's Knowledge Library\n\nThink of Summer as having instant access to a vast game development library. When you're stuck on a technical problem, Summer can research and find the right solution.\n\n**Built-in Godot Expertise**\nSummer knows Godot inside and out. When you ask \"How do I create a third-person camera that follows the player smoothly?\", Summer doesn't guess - it knows the exact Camera3D setup, the right script structure, and the physics considerations.\n\n**Real-Time Research**\nSummer can search current tutorials, documentation, and community resources while you work. Need to implement a specific shader effect? Summer finds the latest examples and adapts them to your project.\n\n**Learning From Your Code**\nSummer analyzes your existing code patterns and suggests improvements. If you have a consistent way of handling player input, Summer learns that pattern and applies it to new features.\n\n**Context-Aware Help**\nUnlike generic AI that gives broad advice, Summer understands your specific game. When you ask for help with collision detection, Summer looks at how you've implemented it elsewhere in your project and gives tailored advice.",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "### What Summer Can Build For You",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "Summer doesn't just talk about game development - it actively builds things in your project. Here's what it can create:",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "**Game Code That Works**",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "Summer writes production-ready GDScript that integrates with your existing codebase. Not just snippets, but complete systems:",
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "text": "- Character controllers with proper physics integration",
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "- Inventory systems with UI and data persistence",
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "text": "- AI behaviors that work with your game's mechanics",
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "text": "- Network synchronization for multiplayer features",
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "text": "**Visual Assets You Need**",
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "text": "When you need game art but don't have an artist yet:",
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "text": "- UI textures and button graphics",
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": "- Environment backgrounds and tilesets",
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "text": "- Character sprites and animations",
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "text": "- Special effects and particle textures",
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "text": "**Audio That Fits Your Game**",
            "lineNumber": 64
          },
          {
            "text": "Sound design that's tailored to your game's atmosphere:",
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "text": "- Background music that matches your game's mood",
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "text": "- Sound effects for specific game actions",
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "text": "- Ambient audio loops for different environments",
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "text": "- Voice acting prompts for professional recording",
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "text": "**Documentation That Helps**",
            "lineNumber": 72
          },
          {
            "text": "Summer creates living documentation that stays current:",
            "lineNumber": 73
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 74
          },
          {
            "text": "- Code comments that explain complex systems",
            "lineNumber": 75
          },
          {
            "text": "- Design documents that capture your game vision",
            "lineNumber": 76
          },
          {
            "text": "- Technical guides for your team members",
            "lineNumber": 77
          },
          {
            "text": "- Player-facing content like tutorials and lore",
            "lineNumber": 78
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "text": "### Summer's Knowledge Library",
            "lineNumber": 80
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81
          },
          {
            "text": "Think of Summer as having instant access to a vast game development library. When you're stuck on a technical problem, Summer can research and find the right solution.",
            "lineNumber": 82
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83
          },
          {
            "text": "**Built-in Godot Expertise**",
            "lineNumber": 84
          },
          {
            "text": "Summer knows Godot inside and out. When you ask \"How do I create a third-person camera that follows the player smoothly?\", Summer doesn't guess - it knows the exact Camera3D setup, the right script structure, and the physics considerations.",
            "lineNumber": 85
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86
          },
          {
            "text": "**Real-Time Research**",
            "lineNumber": 87
          },
          {
            "text": "Summer can search current tutorials, documentation, and community resources while you work. Need to implement a specific shader effect? Summer finds the latest examples and adapts them to your project.",
            "lineNumber": 88
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "text": "**Learning From Your Code**",
            "lineNumber": 90
          },
          {
            "text": "Summer analyzes your existing code patterns and suggests improvements. If you have a consistent way of handling player input, Summer learns that pattern and applies it to new features.",
            "lineNumber": 91
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92
          },
          {
            "text": "**Context-Aware Help**",
            "lineNumber": 93
          },
          {
            "text": "Unlike generic AI that gives broad advice, Summer understands your specific game. When you ask for help with collision detection, Summer looks at how you've implemented it elsewhere in your project and gives tailored advice.",
            "lineNumber": 94
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.2772817015647888
    },

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./essentials/limitations.mdx",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 70
          }
        },
        "contents": "---\ntitle: \"Current Focus &amp; Roadmap\"\ndescription: \"What Summer excels at and what we're building next\"\nicon: \"info\"\n---\n\n## What Summer Does Today\n\nSummer is built for production game development. Here's what works well and what we're improving.\n\n&lt;Note&gt;\nWe ship updates frequently based on developer feedback. Your input shapes the roadmap.\n&lt;/Note&gt;\n\n## Project Intelligence\n\n### Project Indexing\n\nEach project has its own isolated search index. When you open a project, Summer indexes your scripts, scenes, and assets to enable AI-powered search and understanding.\n\n### File Discovery\n\n**What works**: Finds all scripts, scenes, and assets in your project\n\n**Limitation**: Re-indexes daily, not on every file save\n\n**Impact**: Very new files might not appear in search immediately\n\n**Workaround**: Ask the AI to \"explore the project structure\" to find recent files\n\n## Authentication &amp; Desktop Integration\n\n### Authentication Flow\n\n**What works**: One-time login, persistent across app restarts\n\n**Limitation**: Login happens in embedded browser (some OAuth providers may block)\n\n**Workaround**: Use email/password if OAuth fails\n\n### File Operations\n\n**What works**: Add nodes, set properties, connect signals, read any file\n\n**Limitation**: Large file operations (&gt;10MB) may be slow\n\n**Impact**: AI will ask for confirmation before large operations\n\n## AI Chat Features\n\n### Tool Calls\n\n**What works**: Editor operations, file reading, project exploration, grep search across the codebase\n\n**Shell commands**: Always require your approval before any command runs. The agent can propose commands but nothing executes until you confirm.\n\n**Limitation**: Some operations require explicit scene/parent paths\n\n**Impact**: AI may ask follow-up questions for clarity\n\n**Benefit**: Ensures safe, predictable operations\n\n### Model Selection\n\n**What works**: All supported models work with Summer-specific tools\n\n**Limitation**: Tool calls not available with reasoning models\n\n**Workaround**: Switch to standard models for editor operations",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./publishing/steam.mdx",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 75,
            "column": 4
          }
        },
        "contents": "---\ntitle: \"Publishing to Steam\"\ndescription: \"Complete guide to releasing your game on Steam with Summer Engine\"\nicon: \"steam\"\n---\n\n## Overview\n\nSteam is the **largest PC gaming platform** with 120+ million active users. This guide walks you through uploading your Summer Engine game to Steam.\n\n**Prerequisites**:\n- Exported game builds for your target platforms ([macOS](/publishing/macos-export), [Windows](/publishing/windows-export))\n- Steamworks developer account\n- $100 Steam Direct fee (one-time per game)\n\n---\n\n## Before You Begin: Steamworks Setup\n\n### 1. Create Steam Partner Account\n\n&lt;Steps&gt;\n  &lt;Step title=\"Sign Up\"&gt;\n    Visit [Steamworks](https://partner.steamgames.com/) and create an account\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n\n  &lt;Step title=\"Complete Tax Forms\"&gt;\n    Required by Valve for revenue distribution (W-8/W-9 forms)\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n\n  &lt;Step title=\"Pay Steam Direct Fee\"&gt;\n    $100 per game (one-time, recouped after $1,000 in sales)\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n\n  &lt;Step title=\"Create App\"&gt;\n    Click **Create New App** in Steamworks dashboard\n    \n    Fill in basic info (game name, description, genre)\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n&lt;/Steps&gt;\n\n&lt;Info&gt;\n  The Steam Direct fee is **refundable** after your game makes $1,000 in sales. It's Valve's anti-spam measure.\n&lt;/Info&gt;\n\n### 2. Configure Your Steam Store Page\n\nBefore uploading builds, set up your store page:\n- Game description (short and long)\n- Screenshots (at least 5)\n- Trailer video (highly recommended)\n- Pricing and release date\n- Supported languages\n- System requirements\n\n**You can do this while developing** - store pages can be \"Coming Soon\" for wishlists.\n\n---\n\n## Uploading Your Game to Steam\n\n### Understanding Steam Depots\n\nSteam uses \"depots\" for different platform builds:\n\n- **Windows Depot** - Your &#96;MyGame.exe&#96; build\n- **macOS Depot** - Your &#96;MyGame.app&#96; build  \n- **Linux Depot** - Your &#96;MyGame.x86_64&#96; build\n\nEach depot is uploaded separately but players download the right one automatically.\n\n### Upload Process\n\n&lt;Steps&gt;\n  &lt;Step title=\"Install Steamworks SDK\"&gt;\n    ",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "text": "title: \"Publishing to Steam\"",
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "description: \"Complete guide to releasing your game on Steam with Summer Engine\"",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "icon: \"steam\"",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "## Overview",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "Steam is the **largest PC gaming platform** with 120+ million active users. This guide walks you through uploading your Summer Engine game to Steam.",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "**Prerequisites**:",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "- Exported game builds for your target platforms ([macOS](/publishing/macos-export), [Windows](/publishing/windows-export))",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "- Steamworks developer account",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "- $100 Steam Direct fee (one-time per game)",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "## Before You Begin: Steamworks Setup",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "### 1. Create Steam Partner Account",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;Steps&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;Step title=\"Sign Up\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "    Visit [Steamworks](https://partner.steamgames.com/) and create an account",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;/Step&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;Step title=\"Complete Tax Forms\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "    Required by Valve for revenue distribution (W-8/W-9 forms)",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;/Step&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;Step title=\"Pay Steam Direct Fee\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "    $100 per game (one-time, recouped after $1,000 in sales)",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;/Step&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;Step title=\"Create App\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "    Click **Create New App** in Steamworks dashboard",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "    Fill in basic info (game name, description, genre)",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;/Step&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/Steps&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;Info&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "  The Steam Direct fee is **refundable** after your game makes $1,000 in sales. It's Valve's anti-spam measure.",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/Info&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "### 2. Configure Your Steam Store Page",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "Before uploading builds, set up your store page:",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "- Game description (short and long)",
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "- Screenshots (at least 5)",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "text": "- Trailer video (highly recommended)",
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "- Pricing and release date",
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "text": "- Supported languages",
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "text": "- System requirements",
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "text": "**You can do this while developing** - store pages can be \"Coming Soon\" for wishlists.",
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": "## Uploading Your Game to Steam",
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "text": "### Understanding Steam Depots",
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "text": "Steam uses \"depots\" for different platform builds:",
            "lineNumber": 64
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Windows Depot** - Your &#96;MyGame.exe&#96; build",
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "text": "- **macOS Depot** - Your &#96;MyGame.app&#96; build  ",
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Linux Depot** - Your &#96;MyGame.x86_64&#96; build",
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "text": "Each depot is uploaded separately but players download the right one automatically.",
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {

        ]

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./ai-tools/operations.mdx",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 88
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 131
          }
        },
        "contents": "**Learning From Your Code**\nSummer analyzes your existing code patterns and suggests improvements. If you have a consistent way of handling player input, Summer learns that pattern and applies it to new features.\n\n**Context-Aware Help**\nUnlike generic AI that gives broad advice, Summer understands your specific game. When you ask for help with collision detection, Summer looks at how you've implemented it elsewhere in your project and gives tailored advice.\n\n### Working Directly in Your Project\n\nSummer doesn't just suggest changes - it can implement them directly in your Godot project. This means you can describe what you want and see it appear in your game.\n\n**Safe File Operations**\nSummer creates and modifies files with care. When you ask Summer to \"add a health system to my player\", it:\n\n1. Creates a new script file with the health logic\n2. Adds the necessary variables and functions\n3. Connects it to your existing player scene\n4. Sets up any required UI elements\n\n**Smart Project Understanding**\nSummer knows how your project is organized. It understands:\n- Which scenes connect to which scripts\n- How your assets are structured\n- What naming conventions you use\n- How your game's systems work together\n\n**Seamless Asset Integration**\nWhen Summer generates an asset for you (like a texture or sound), it automatically places it in the right folder and connects it to your scenes. No manual importing required.\n\n**Undo-Able Everything**\nEvery change Summer makes can be undone with Ctrl+Z. If something doesn't work as expected, you can instantly revert it and try a different approach.\n\n## Which AI Models Power Summer?\n\nSummer integrates with leading AI providers to give you the best experience for different tasks.\n\n### Primary AI Models\n**Claude 4.5 Sonnet (Anthropic)** - Default model for code generation and project assistance\n- Latest Claude model with superior reasoning for complex programming\n- Best for game logic, debugging, and architecture decisions\n\n**GPT-5 (OpenAI)** - Alternative model for creative and documentation tasks\n- Most advanced GPT model with multimodal capabilities",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "**Learning From Your Code**",
            "lineNumber": 90
          },
          {
            "text": "Summer analyzes your existing code patterns and suggests improvements. If you have a consistent way of handling player input, Summer learns that pattern and applies it to new features.",
            "lineNumber": 91
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92
          },
          {
            "text": "**Context-Aware Help**",
            "lineNumber": 93
          },
          {
            "text": "Unlike generic AI that gives broad advice, Summer understands your specific game. When you ask for help with collision detection, Summer looks at how you've implemented it elsewhere in your project and gives tailored advice.",
            "lineNumber": 94
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95
          },
          {
            "text": "### Working Directly in Your Project",
            "lineNumber": 96
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97
          },
          {
            "text": "Summer doesn't just suggest changes - it can implement them directly in your Godot project. This means you can describe what you want and see it appear in your game.",
            "lineNumber": 98
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99
          },
          {
            "text": "**Safe File Operations**",
            "lineNumber": 100
          },
          {
            "text": "Summer creates and modifies files with care. When you ask Summer to \"add a health system to my player\", it:",
            "lineNumber": 101
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102
          },
          {
            "text": "1. Creates a new script file with the health logic",
            "lineNumber": 103
          },
          {
            "text": "2. Adds the necessary variables and functions",
            "lineNumber": 104
          },
          {
            "text": "3. Connects it to your existing player scene",
            "lineNumber": 105
          },
          {
            "text": "4. Sets up any required UI elements",
            "lineNumber": 106
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107
          },
          {
            "text": "**Smart Project Understanding**",
            "lineNumber": 108
          },
          {
            "text": "Summer knows how your project is organized. It understands:",
            "lineNumber": 109
          },
          {
            "text": "- Which scenes connect to which scripts",
            "lineNumber": 110
          },
          {
            "text": "- How your assets are structured",
            "lineNumber": 111
          },
          {
            "text": "- What naming conventions you use",
            "lineNumber": 112
          },
          {
            "text": "- How your game's systems work together",
            "lineNumber": 113
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 114
          },
          {
            "text": "**Seamless Asset Integration**",
            "lineNumber": 115
          },
          {
            "text": "When Summer generates an asset for you (like a texture or sound), it automatically places it in the right folder and connects it to your scenes. No manual importing required.",
            "lineNumber": 116
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117
          },
          {
            "text": "**Undo-Able Everything**",
            "lineNumber": 118
          },
          {
            "text": "Every change Summer makes can be undone with Ctrl+Z. If something doesn't work as expected, you can instantly revert it and try a different approach.",
            "lineNumber": 119
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120
          },
          {
            "text": "## Which AI Models Power Summer?",
            "lineNumber": 121
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 122
          },
          {
            "text": "Summer integrates with leading AI providers to give you the best experience for different tasks.",
            "lineNumber": 123
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 124
          },
          {
            "text": "### Primary AI Models",
            "lineNumber": 125
          },
          {
            "text": "**Claude 4.5 Sonnet (Anthropic)** - Default model for code generation and project assistance",
            "lineNumber": 126
          },
          {
            "text": "- Latest Claude model with superior reasoning for complex programming",
            "lineNumber": 127
          },
          {
            "text": "- Best for game logic, debugging, and architecture decisions",
            "lineNumber": 128
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 129
          },
          {
            "text": "**GPT-5 (OpenAI)** - Alternative model for creative and documentation tasks",
            "lineNumber": 130
          },
          {
            "text": "- Most advanced GPT model with multimodal capabilities",
            "lineNumber": 131
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.2703601121902466
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./art-system/overview.mdx",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 118
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 160
          }
        },
        "contents": "- **Artist Attribution**: Human artists get full credit and recognition\n- **AI Disclosure**: AI-generated content is never hidden or misrepresented\n\n### Artist Support\n- **Fair Compensation**: Revenue sharing for commercial licenses on human-created assets\n- **Portfolio Building**: Artists can showcase their work and build their reputation\n- **Community Recognition**: Highlighting exceptional human creativity and craftsmanship\n- **Anti-Theft Protection**: Tools to report unauthorized use of human artwork\n\n### Ethical AI Use\n- **Prototyping Focus**: AI tools positioned as prototyping aids, not final art solutions\n- **Style Respect**: AI generation avoids mimicking specific living artists' styles\n- **Education**: Resources on when to use AI vs. hiring human artists\n- **Transition Support**: Help developers move from AI prototypes to human-created final art\n\n## Integration with Summer\n\nAssets from the Summer Asset Store integrate seamlessly with Summer's game development workflow:\n\n- **Direct Import**: Import assets directly into your Summer projects\n- **AI Context**: Summer's AI understands your asset choices for better suggestions\n- **Project Organization**: Assets are automatically organized in your project structure\n- **Version Control**: Asset changes are tracked with your project history\n\n## Next Steps\n\n&lt;CardGroup cols={2}&gt;\n  &lt;Card\n    title=\"Start Creating\"\n    icon=\"plus\"\n    href=\"/art-system/ai-generation\"\n  &gt;\n    Generate your first AI-powered game asset\n  &lt;/Card&gt;\n  &lt;Card\n    title=\"Explore Gallery\"\n    icon=\"compass\"\n    href=\"/art-system/gallery\"\n  &gt;\n    Browse the community asset library\n  &lt;/Card&gt;\n&lt;/CardGroup&gt;",
        "signatures": {},

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./development/architecture.mdx",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 72
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 151

        },
        "contents": "- **No Code Storage**: Your code is never stored on our servers\n- **Anonymized Telemetry**: Only usage patterns, never code content\n\n### Safe Operations\n- **Undo System**: All changes integrate with editor undo\n- **Validation**: AI changes are validated before application\n- **Sandboxing**: AI operations run in isolated contexts\n- **User Approval**: Complex changes require explicit confirmation\n\n## Performance Optimizations\n\n### Local Processing\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nProject Indexing (Local)\n├── AST Parsing → Symbol Tables\n├── Dependency Analysis → Call Graphs  \n├── Pattern Recognition → Style Guides\n└── Caching → Fast Retrieval\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Cloud Efficiency\n- **Request Batching**: Multiple operations combined\n- **Smart Caching**: Frequently used patterns cached\n- **Edge Computing**: Servers close to users\n- **Load Balancing**: Automatic scaling\n\n## Plugin Architecture\n\nSummer supports extensions through a plugin system:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;gdscript\n# Example plugin structure\nextends SummerPlugin\n\nfunc _ready():\n    register_command(\"my_command\", handle_command)\n    add_menu_item(\"Tools/My Plugin\", show_dialog)\n\nfunc handle_command(args: Dictionary):\n    # Custom AI command logic\n    pass\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Plugin Capabilities\n- **Custom AI Commands**: Add domain-specific operations\n- **UI Extensions**: New panels and dialogs\n- **File Handlers**: Support for additional file types\n- **Integration Hooks**: Connect to external tools\n\n## Development\n\nSummer is built with a modular architecture designed for performance and maintainability.\n\n### Development Setup\n\n&lt;CodeGroup&gt;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash Clone &amp; Build\n# Clone the repository\ngit clone https://github.com/SummerEngine/Summer.git\ncd Summer\n\n# Install dependencies\n./scripts/setup.sh\n\n# Build debug version\nscons platform=linux target=editor dev=yes\n\n# Run Summer\n./bin/summer.linux.editor.dev.x86_64\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash Plugin Development\n# Create new plugin\n./scripts/create_plugin.sh my_plugin\n\n# Install for development\nln -s $(pwd)/plugins/my_plugin ~/.summer/plugins/",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {

          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 75

          {

          },
          {
            "text": "- **Validation**: AI changes are validated before application",

          {
            "text": "- **Sandboxing**: AI operations run in isolated contexts",
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "text": "- **User Approval**: Complex changes require explicit confirmation",
            "lineNumber": 80

            "lineNumber": 81

          {
            "text": "├── AST Parsing → Symbol Tables",

          {
            "lineNumber": 92
          },
          {

          {
            "lineNumber": 98
          },
          {
            "text": "## Plugin Architecture",

          {

            "lineNumber": 121

          {

          {

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./development/architecture.mdx",
        "range": {

        "contents": "---\ntitle: \"Summer Architecture\"\ndescription: \"Technical overview of how Summer works\"\nicon: \"sitemap\"\n---\n\n## System Overview\n\nSummer is built as a hybrid desktop application with cloud-enhanced AI capabilities.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\ngraph TB\n    A[Summer Desktop App] --&gt; B[Local Project Manager]\n    A --&gt; C[AI Chat Interface]\n    A --&gt; D[Scene Editor]\n    \n    C --&gt; E[Cloud AI Service]\n    E --&gt; F[Code Generation]\n    E --&gt; G[Project Analysis]\n    \n    B --&gt; H[Local File System]\n    B --&gt; I[Version Control]\n    \n    D --&gt; J[Rendering Engine]\n    D --&gt; K[Physics Engine]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Core Components\n\n### Desktop Application\n- **Framework**: Built with Godot Engine for native performance\n- **UI**: Custom editor interface with dockable panels\n- **Platform**: Cross-platform (Windows, macOS, Linux)\n\n### AI Integration\n- **Local Analysis**: Project indexing happens on your machine\n- **Cloud Processing**: Complex AI operations use cloud GPUs\n- **Hybrid Approach**: Sensitive code never leaves your computer\n\n### Project Management\n- **File Watching**: Real-time monitoring of project changes\n- **Version Control**: Git integration with automatic commits\n- **Backup System**: Automatic project backups before AI changes\n\n## AI Pipeline\n\n&lt;Steps&gt;\n  &lt;Step title=\"Input Processing\"&gt;\n    User message is parsed and analyzed for intent\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n  &lt;Step title=\"Context Gathering\"&gt;\n    Relevant project files and structure are identified\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n  &lt;Step title=\"Plan Generation\"&gt;\n    AI creates a step-by-step plan for the requested changes\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n  &lt;Step title=\"Code Generation\"&gt;\n    Specific code and scene modifications are generated\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n  &lt;Step title=\"Validation\"&gt;\n    Changes are validated against project constraints\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n  &lt;Step title=\"Application\"&gt;\n    Changes are applied through the editor's APIs\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n&lt;/Steps&gt;\n\n## Security Model\n\n### Data Privacy\n- **Local First**: All project analysis happens locally\n- **Encrypted Transit**: API calls use TLS 1.3\n- **No Code Storage**: Your code is never stored on our servers\n- **Anonymized Telemetry**: Only usage patterns, never code content",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "text": "title: \"Summer Architecture\"",
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "description: \"Technical overview of how Summer works\"",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "icon: \"sitemap\"",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "## System Overview",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "Summer is built as a hybrid desktop application with cloud-enhanced AI capabilities.",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "graph TB",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "    A[Summer Desktop App] --&gt; B[Local Project Manager]",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "    A --&gt; C[AI Chat Interface]",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "    A --&gt; D[Scene Editor]",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "    C --&gt; E[Cloud AI Service]",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "    E --&gt; F[Code Generation]",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "    E --&gt; G[Project Analysis]",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "    B --&gt; H[Local File System]",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "    B --&gt; I[Version Control]",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "    D --&gt; J[Rendering Engine]",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "    D --&gt; K[Physics Engine]",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "## Core Components",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "### Desktop Application",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Framework**: Built with Godot Engine for native performance",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "- **UI**: Custom editor interface with dockable panels",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Platform**: Cross-platform (Windows, macOS, Linux)",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "### AI Integration",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Local Analysis**: Project indexing happens on your machine",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Cloud Processing**: Complex AI operations use cloud GPUs",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Hybrid Approach**: Sensitive code never leaves your computer",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "### Project Management",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "- **File Watching**: Real-time monitoring of project changes",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Version Control**: Git integration with automatic commits",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Backup System**: Automatic project backups before AI changes",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "## AI Pipeline",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;Steps&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;Step title=\"Input Processing\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "    User message is parsed and analyzed for intent",
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;/Step&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;Step title=\"Context Gathering\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "    Relevant project files and structure are identified",
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;/Step&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;Step title=\"Plan Generation\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "text": "    AI creates a step-by-step plan for the requested changes",
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;/Step&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;Step title=\"Code Generation\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "text": "    Specific code and scene modifications are generated",
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;/Step&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;Step title=\"Validation\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "text": "    Changes are validated against project constraints",
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;/Step&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;Step title=\"Application\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "text": "    Changes are applied through the editor's APIs",
            "lineNumber": 64
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;/Step&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/Steps&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "text": "## Security Model",
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "text": "### Data Privacy",
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Local First**: All project analysis happens locally",
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Encrypted Transit**: API calls use TLS 1.3",
            "lineNumber": 72
          },
          {
            "text": "- **No Code Storage**: Your code is never stored on our servers",
            "lineNumber": 73
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Anonymized Telemetry**: Only usage patterns, never code content",
            "lineNumber": 74
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.2670876085758209
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./publishing/macos-export.mdx",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 78
          }
        },
        "contents": "---\ntitle: \"macOS Export Guide\"\ndescription: \"Step-by-step guide to exporting your game for Mac computers\"\nicon: \"apple\"\n---\n\n## Overview\n\nThis guide walks you through exporting your Summer Engine game for macOS. Whether you're targeting Steam, itch.io, or direct distribution, the export process is the same.\n\n**Time to complete**: 10-15 minutes (first time)\n\n## Prerequisites\n\n### Enable ETC2 ASTC Texture Compression\n\nApple Silicon Macs (M1, M2, M3 chips) require ETC2/ASTC texture format.\n\n&lt;Steps&gt;\n  &lt;Step title=\"Open Project Settings\"&gt;\n    Go to **Project → Project Settings** in the menu bar\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n\n  &lt;Step title=\"Navigate to Textures\"&gt;\n    **Rendering → Textures → VRAM Compression**\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n\n  &lt;Step title=\"Enable ETC2 ASTC\"&gt;\n    Toggle **Import ETC2 ASTC** to **On**\n    \n    &lt;img src=\"/images/publishing/enable-etc2-astc.png\" alt=\"Enable ETC2 ASTC\" /&gt;\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n\n  &lt;Step title=\"Wait for Reimport\"&gt;\n    Summer will reimport all textures (1-5 minutes depending on project size)\n    \n    You'll see progress in the bottom status bar. **Do not close Summer** during this process.\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n&lt;/Steps&gt;\n\n&lt;Tip&gt;\n  **Also releasing on Windows?** Enable **Import S3TC BPTC** at the same time. This covers both Mac and Windows with one reimport.\n&lt;/Tip&gt;\n\n---\n\n## Creating a macOS Export Preset\n\n&lt;Steps&gt;\n  &lt;Step title=\"Open Export Menu\"&gt;\n    Go to **Project → Export** in the menu bar\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n\n  &lt;Step title=\"Add macOS Preset\"&gt;\n    Click **Add...** → Select **macOS (Runnable)**\n    \n    You'll see the export configuration panel appear.\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n\n  &lt;Step title=\"Configure Required Settings\"&gt;\n    You'll see some warnings in red at the bottom. Let's fix them:\n  &lt;/Step&gt;\n&lt;/Steps&gt;\n\n### Fix: Invalid Bundle Identifier\n\n&lt;Warning&gt;\n  You'll see: **\"Invalid Bundle Identifier (com.example.game). The Bundle Identifier should be a valid domain name in reverse...\"**\n&lt;/Warning&gt;\n\n**What is this?** A bundle identifier is a unique name for your app in reverse-domain format.\n\n**Examples**:\n- &#96;com.yourstudio.yourgame&#96; (most common)\n- &#96;com.yourname.dontpray&#96;\n- &#96;io.itch.username.gamename&#96;\n\n**How to fix**:",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "text": "title: \"macOS Export Guide\"",
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "description: \"Step-by-step guide to exporting your game for Mac computers\"",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "icon: \"apple\"",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "## Overview",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "This guide walks you through exporting your Summer Engine game for macOS. Whether you're targeting Steam, itch.io, or direct distribution, the export process is the same.",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "**Time to complete**: 10-15 minutes (first time)",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "## Prerequisites",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "### Enable ETC2 ASTC Texture Compression",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "Apple Silicon Macs (M1, M2, M3 chips) require ETC2/ASTC texture format.",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;Steps&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;Step title=\"Open Project Settings\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "    Go to **Project → Project Settings** in the menu bar",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;/Step&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;Step title=\"Navigate to Textures\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "    **Rendering → Textures → VRAM Compression**",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;/Step&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;Step title=\"Enable ETC2 ASTC\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "    Toggle **Import ETC2 ASTC** to **On**",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "    &lt;img src=\"/images/publishing/enable-etc2-astc.png\" alt=\"Enable ETC2 ASTC\" /&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;/Step&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;Step title=\"Wait for Reimport\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "    Summer will reimport all textures (1-5 minutes depending on project size)",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "    You'll see progress in the bottom status bar. **Do not close Summer** during this process.",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;/Step&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/Steps&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;Tip&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "  **Also releasing on Windows?** Enable **Import S3TC BPTC** at the same time. This covers both Mac and Windows with one reimport.",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/Tip&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "## Creating a macOS Export Preset",
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;Steps&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;Step title=\"Open Export Menu\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "text": "    Go to **Project → Export** in the menu bar",
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;/Step&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;Step title=\"Add macOS Preset\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "text": "    Click **Add...** → Select **macOS (Runnable)**",
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "text": "    You'll see the export configuration panel appear.",
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;/Step&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;Step title=\"Configure Required Settings\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "text": "    You'll see some warnings in red at the bottom. Let's fix them:",
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;/Step&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/Steps&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64
          },
          {
            "text": "### Fix: Invalid Bundle Identifier",
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;Warning&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "text": "  You'll see: **\"Invalid Bundle Identifier (com.example.game). The Bundle Identifier should be a valid domain name in reverse...\"**",
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/Warning&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "text": "**What is this?** A bundle identifier is a unique name for your app in reverse-domain format.",
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 72
          },
          {
            "text": "**Examples**:",
            "lineNumber": 73
          },
          {
            "text": "- &#96;com.yourstudio.yourgame&#96; (most common)",
            "lineNumber": 74
          },
          {
            "text": "- &#96;com.yourname.dontpray&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 75
          },
          {
            "text": "- &#96;io.itch.username.gamename&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 76
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 77

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./art-system/ai-generation.mdx",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 74
